title: Rep. John Talley (R): Keep abortion legal because "we have enough abortion laws"
author: James Silberman
orientation: During a June 2020 OK House District 33 candidate debate, incumbent Republican Rep. John Talley said Oklahoma already has "enough abortion laws," opposed SB13 by citing a tubal-pregnancy scenario the bill explicitly addresses, and signaled that abortifacient drugs like Plan B should remain legal. The article frames Talley as a representative case of pro-life political failure compared to abolitionist challenger Brice Chaffin.
audiences: Oklahoma voter weighing the Talley vs. Chaffin race
Pro-life Republican curious about abolitionist critique
Pastor or pro-life advocate concerned about life-of-the-mother and ectopic-pregnancy objections

questions.question: What did Rep. John Talley say when asked about abolishing abortion at the June 16 debate?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the Talley vs. Chaffin race
questions.answer: Talley flatly stated that Oklahoma already has sufficient abortion legislation and reaffirmed that position when challenger Brice Chaffin asked him to confirm it.
questions.quote: Talley's shocking position on the issue: "We have enough abortion laws right now."

questions.question: Does SB13 actually criminalize doctors who treat life-threatening tubal or ectopic pregnancies the way Talley implied?
questions.audience: Pastor or pro-life advocate concerned about life-of-the-mother and ectopic-pregnancy objections
questions.answer: No. The bill contains an explicit immunity clause for physicians who act in a triage situation to save the mother after exhausting treatments to preserve the fetus, including in tubal or ectopic pregnancies.
questions.quote: "A physician or other health care professional shall be immune from criminal or civil liability for the death of a fetus resulting from an emergency, including but not limited to an ectopic or tubal pregnancy, if the physician or professional exhausts all possible treatments to preserve the life of the fetus."

questions.question: Why does Free the States distinguish between "laws against abortion" and "laws regulating abortion"?
questions.audience: Pro-life Republican curious about abolitionist critique
questions.answer: The article argues that incumbents like Talley defend the status quo as adequate, but the existing legal regime only regulates abortion rather than prohibiting it, leaving thousands of babies legally killed in Oklahoma each year.
questions.quote: The reality is, we don't have laws against abortion. We have laws regulating abortion.

questions.question: What did Talley say about abortifacient drugs like Plan B?
questions.answer: Talley argued that abolishing abortion is problematic because it would affect the legality of Plan B and similar drugs, indicating he wants those drugs to remain legal even though they can cause an abortion after conception.
questions.quote: Talley also explained that "the problem with saying you're going to abolish [abortion]" is that it's going to effect the legality of abortifacient drugs like Plan B which can either prevent conception or cause an abortion after conception has already occurred. Talley apparently wants abortifacient drugs to remain legal as well.

questions.question: How does the article characterize the current legal status of abortion procedures in Oklahoma under Talley's preferred regime?
questions.answer: Silberman describes the status quo as one in which dismemberment abortions are still legal, and he argues Talley considers that legal landscape acceptable.
questions.quote: It is literally legal to tear human beings limb-from-bloody-limb in Oklahoma and Republican incumbent John Talley believes the law is just fine as it is.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Directly addresses the kind of misleading SB13 talking points (including life-of-the-mother claims) that Talley repeated at the debate.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
related.reason: Explains the abolitionist rationale Chaffin endorses, contrasted with Talley's "enough abortion laws" stance.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Documents another Republican lawmaker mischaracterizing SB13, paralleling Talley's false claim that SB13 would have killed his family member.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Part of the same series tracking GOP officials who blocked or undermined SB13 in 2020.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Counters the same category of objections (regulation is enough, abolition is too radical) that Talley voiced in the debate.

related.title: Against Pro-Life Compromise: Responding to Denny Burk, Andrew Walker, et al.
related.reason: Provides the broader theological and ethical refutation of ectopic-pregnancy and abortifacient objections Talley relied on to oppose abolition.
